# validate.switch_density { #tspaint.validate.switch_density }

```python
tspaint.validate.switch_density(segs, length)
```

Ancestry switches per unit length.

This is the quantity admixture-pulse dating reads as time: over-fragmentation
inflates it and biases the inferred pulse older, over-smoothing deflates it
and biases younger.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**segs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Hard segment list ``[(left, right, state)]``.

<code>[**length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sequence length to normalise by.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Number of ancestry switches divided by ``length``, or ``nan`` if ``length <= 0``.
